skill.name: latency-tracker
skill.description: Per-call and aggregated latency tracking for MEV infrastructure. Use when implementing performance monitoring or debugging slow operations. Triggers on: latency, timing, performance, slow, speed, instrumentation.
skill.summary: Per-call and aggregated latency tracking for MEV infrastructure. Use when implementing performance m...
skill.author: BarisSozen

content.user_title: Track MEV Latency Across Code Paths
content.value_statement: Slow MEV operations are hard to diagnose without clear timing spans. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design latency tracking, span hierarchy, and alert thresholds.
actual_capabilities: Defines a span hierarchy from end-to-end flow down to individual operations.
Shows how to wrap operations with tracker span calls.
Suggests latency ranges for Rust hot paths, RPC calls, and submit flows.
Supports debugging slow operations with per-call timing data.
Supports aggregated latency tracking for MEV infrastructure.
limitations: It provides guidance only and does not include an executable tracker library.
It does not include dashboard, storage, or alert delivery integrations.
The thresholds are examples and may need tuning for each system.
It does not include benchmark scripts or automated tests.

use_cases.title: Instrument a Trading Path
use_cases.description: Add spans around hot path calls, RPC calls, transaction submission, and confirmation timing.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Slow Operations
use_cases.description: Map a slow end-to-end operation into measurable spans and identify where latency is added.

use_cases.title: Plan Latency Alerts
use_cases.description: Convert expected timing ranges into alert thresholds for production monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Timing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the latency-tracker skill to design simple per-call timing for this operation. Explain the span names and expected timing ranges.

prompt_templates.title: Build Span Hierarchy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the latency-tracker skill to create a span hierarchy for my MEV flow. Include root, hot path, RPC, submit, and confirm spans.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Slow End-to-End Latency
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced troubleshooting for a slow path.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the latency-tracker skill to analyze this slow end-to-end flow. Identify likely bottlenecks and propose span-level measurements.

prompt_templates.title: Design Production Alerts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the latency-tracker skill to design alert thresholds for production latency monitoring. Include expected ranges, warning levels, and critical levels.

output_examples.input: Help me instrument a liquidation flow with latency spans.
output_examples.output: A structured span plan with an end-to-end root span, Rust hot path span, RPC span, submit span, and confirmation span.

output_examples.input: My RPC calls sometimes add 80 milliseconds. What should I monitor?
output_examples.output: A monitoring recommendation that compares RPC timing against expected ranges and proposes alert thresholds for repeated slow calls.

output_examples.input: Create a latency debugging plan for a slow transaction path.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step diagnostic plan that separates hot path work, network calls, transaction submission, and confirmation time.

best_practices: Name spans consistently so aggregated reports are easy to compare.
Measure both per-call latency and end-to-end latency for each flow.
Tune alert thresholds with production baseline data before paging operators.
anti_patterns: Using one broad timer for the whole flow without child spans.
Copying example thresholds without checking real system latency.
Recording latency data without linking it to operation success or failure.

faq.question: Does this skill install a latency tracker?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for designing and adding latency instrumentation.

faq.question: Is this skill only for MEV systems?
faq.answer: It is written for MEV infrastructure, but the span approach can apply to other latency-sensitive systems.

faq.question: Can it create alert thresholds?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help propose thresholds based on expected latency ranges and production goals.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. It is marked as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it include storage or dashboards?
faq.answer: No. You need to connect the instrumentation plan to your own metrics and observability stack.

faq.question: What should I provide in a prompt?
faq.answer: Provide the operation flow, expected latency ranges, slow symptoms, and current monitoring gaps.
